reasoning:
The question asks whether the US Treasury General Account (WDTGAL) will increase between Wednesday, March 11, 2026 (the value available on March 15) and Wednesday, March 18, 2026 (the value available on March 22). Historically, the TGA trends downwards during February and March because of the heavy volume of individual tax refunds being paid by the IRS. While mid-month Treasury auction settlements (which would occur on Monday, March 16, resulting from auctions the prior week) provide some cash influx, there is no major corporate tax deadline on March 15 (S-corps and partnerships file informational returns, with the actual tax paid by individuals on April 15). 

Looking at recent data from the FRED fetch, the TGA has been steadily declining during this tax refund season: 
- Feb 4: 907k
- Feb 11: 910k
- Feb 18: 888k
- Feb 25: 838k
- Mar 4: 846k
- Mar 11: 805k

The strong downward momentum from tax refund payments, without a corresponding large corporate tax inflow (like the one seen in April, June, September, and December), suggests the TGA is more likely to continue its seasonal decline over the mid-March week. Thus, the probability of a week-over-week increase is lower than a coin flip.
